revision.text: #redirect[[en:Prince_Kume]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳－603年）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。父は[[用明天皇]]。[[聖徳太子]]は兄。
[[602年]]、[[新羅]]を撃つため[[将軍]]に任じられ、軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至ったが、[[病気]]に罹り新羅への進軍を延期とした。翌[[603年]][[2月]]皇子は筑紫国で没したため、新羅への侵攻は取りやめとなった。
墓所は、[[河内国]]埴生山岡（現在の[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）。
[[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] {{people-stub}}

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - 推古天皇11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。

墓所は、[[河内国]]埴生山岡（現在の[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）。
[[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] [[Category:603年没|くめのみこ]] {{people-stub}}

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳（一説に[[577年]]） - 推古天皇11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。
[[602年]]、[[新羅]]を撃つため[[将軍]]に任じられ、軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至ったが、[[病気]]に罹り新羅への進軍を延期とした。翌[[603年]]2月4日皇子は筑紫国で没したため、新羅への侵攻は取りやめとなった。
墓所は、[[河内国]]埴生山岡（現在の[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）。
= 系譜 = *父：[[用明天皇]] *母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] *兄弟姉妹：[[聖徳太子]]・殖栗皇子・茨田皇子・佐冨女王（父は田目皇子）
*異母兄弟姉妹：田目皇子・麻呂子皇子・酸香手姫皇女
*甥・姪：山背大兄王・財王・日置王・片岡女王・白髪部王・手島女王・長谷王・三枝王・伊止志古王・麻呂古王・春米女王・久波太女王・波止利女王・馬屋古女王
[[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] [[Category:603年没|くめのみこ]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳（一説に[[577年]]） - 推古天皇11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。 [[602年]]、[[新羅]]を撃つため[[将軍]]に任じられ、軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至ったが、[[病気]]に罹り新羅への進軍を延期とした。翌[[603年]]2月4日皇子は筑紫国で没したため、新羅への侵攻は取りやめとなった。 墓所は、[[河内国]]埴生山岡（現在の[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）。 = 系譜 = [[用明天皇]]皇子。母は欽明天皇の皇女・[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。 *兄弟姉妹：[[聖徳太子]]・殖栗皇子・茨田皇子 *異母兄弟姉妹：田目皇子・[[麻呂子皇子]]・[[酢香手姫皇女]] *異父兄弟姉妹：佐富女王（父は田目皇子）
*妃：由波利王（ゆばりのみこ、桜井弓張皇女。[[敏達天皇]]の皇女） **男王（おとこのみこ） **星河女王（ほしかわのひめみこ） **佐富王（さほのみこ） *妃：比里古郎女（ひろこのいらつめ。[[膳傾子]]の女、[[菩岐岐美郎女]]の妹） **高椅王（たかはしのみこ） *甥・姪：[[山背大兄王]]・財王・日置王・片岡女王・白髪部王・手島女王・[[泊瀬仲王|長谷王]]・三枝王・伊止志古王・麻呂古王・春米女王・久波太女王・波止利女王・馬屋古女王 *子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など [[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] [[Category:603年没|くめのみこ]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳（一説に[[577年]]） - 推古天皇11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。 [[602年]]、[[新羅]]を撃つため[[将軍]]に任じられ、軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至ったが、[[病気]]に罹り新羅への進軍を延期とした。翌[[603年]]2月4日皇子は筑紫国で没したため、新羅への侵攻は取りやめとなった。 墓所は、[[河内国]]埴生山岡（現在の[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）。 = 系譜 = [[用明天皇]]皇子。母は欽明天皇の皇女・[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。 *兄弟姉妹：[[聖徳太子]]・殖栗皇子・茨田皇子 *異母兄弟姉妹：田目皇子・[[麻呂子皇子]]・[[酢香手姫皇女]] *異父兄弟姉妹：佐富女王（父は田目皇子）
*妃：由波利王（ゆばりのみこ。桜井弓張皇女とも）- [[敏達天皇]]皇女 **男王（おとこのみこ） **星河女王（ほしかわのひめみこ） **佐富王（さほのみこ） *妃：比里古郎女（ひろこのいらつめ） - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **高椅王（たかはしのみこ） *甥・姪：[[山背大兄王]]・財王・日置王・片岡女王・白髪部王・手島女王・[[泊瀬仲王|長谷王]]・三枝王・伊止志古王・麻呂古王・春米女王・久波太女王・波止利女王・馬屋古女王 *子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など [[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] [[Category:603年没|くめのみこ]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[新羅]]征討[[将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、[[筑紫国|筑紫]]にて薨去。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]（もがり）し、[[土師猪手]]（はじのいて）がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの三丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 = 系譜 = [[用明天皇]]皇子。母は欽明天皇の皇女・[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。 *兄弟姉妹：[[聖徳太子]]・殖栗皇子・茨田皇子 *異母兄弟姉妹：田目皇子・[[麻呂子皇子]]・[[酢香手姫皇女]] *異父兄弟姉妹：佐富女王（父は田目皇子）
*妃：由波利王（ゆばりのみこ。桜井弓張皇女とも）- [[敏達天皇]]皇女 **男王（おとこのみこ） **星河女王（ほしかわのひめみこ） **佐富王（さほのみこ） *妃：比里古郎女（ひろこのいらつめ） - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **高椅王（たかはしのみこ） *甥・姪：[[山背大兄王]]・財王・日置王・片岡女王・白髪部王・手島女王・[[泊瀬仲王|長谷王]]・三枝王・伊止志古王・麻呂古王・春米女王・久波太女王・波止利女王・馬屋古女王 *子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など [[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族|くめのみこ]] [[Category:603年没|くめのみこ]]

推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[新羅]]征討[[将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (筑前国)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]（もがり）し、[[土師猪手]]（はじのいて）がこれを管掌した。
[[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの三丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。
== 系譜 == *父：[[用明天皇]]皇子 *母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女 *同母兄弟姉妹：[[聖徳太子]]・殖栗皇子・茨田皇子
*妃：由波利王（ゆばりのみこ。桜井弓張皇女とも）- [[敏達天皇]]皇女 **男王（おとこのみこ） **星河女王（ほしかわのひめみこ） **佐富王（さほのみこ） *妃：比里古郎女（ひろこのいらつめ） - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **高椅王（たかはしのみこ）
*子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など
{{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族]] [[Category:603年没]]

*妃：由波利王（ゆばりのみこ。桜井弓張皇女とも）- [[敏達天皇]]皇女 **男子：男王（おとこのみこ） **女子：星河女王（ほしかわのひめみこ） **男子：佐富王（さほのみこ） *妃：比里古郎女（ひろこのいらつめ） - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **男子：高椅王（たかはしのみこ） *生母不明 **男子：日田王<ref>宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会,1986年</ref> - 子孫は登美真人

*妃：由波利王<ref>読みは「ゆばりのみこ」</ref>（桜井弓張皇女とも）- [[敏達天皇]]皇女 **男子：男王<ref>読みは「おとこのみこ」</ref> **女子：星河女王<ref>読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」</ref> **男子：佐富王<ref>読みは「さほのみこ」</ref> *妃：比里古郎女<ref>読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」</ref> - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **男子：高椅王<ref>読みは「たかはしのみこ」</ref> *生母不明 **男子：日田王<ref>中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会,1986年 による）</ref> - 子孫は登美[[真人]]
:*子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など
== 関連項目 == *[[久米寺]]・・・来目王子が開基とされる。 *[[久留米市]]・・・地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 *[[新羅征討計画]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥・奈良時代の皇族]] [[Category:603年没]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。
推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]の際、[[新羅]]征討[[将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (筑前国)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。
[[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの三丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。

:*子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。

*妃：由波利王<ref>読みは「ゆばりのみこ」</ref>（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]]皇女 **男子：男王<ref>読みは「おとこのみこ」</ref> **女子：星河女王<ref>読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」</ref> **男子：佐富王<ref>読みは「さほのみこ」</ref> *妃：比里古郎女<ref>読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」</ref> - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **男子：高椅王<ref>読みは「たかはしのみこ」</ref> *生母不明 **男子：日田王<ref>中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会,1986年 による）</ref> - 子孫は登美[[真人]]

'''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。
== 略歴 == 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]の際、[[新羅]]征討[[将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (筑前国)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて薨去。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。
[[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。
== 系譜<small><ref>日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。</ref></small> == *父：[[用明天皇]] *母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 *同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]]
*妃：由波利王<ref>読みは「ゆばりのみこ」</ref>（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]]皇女 **男子：男王<ref>読みは「おとこのみこ」</ref> **女子：星河女王<ref>読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」</ref> **男子：佐富王<ref>読みは「さほのみこ」</ref> *妃：比里古郎女<ref>読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」</ref> - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **男子：高椅王<ref>読みは「たかはしのみこ」</ref> *生母不明 **男子：日田王<ref>中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会,1986年 による）</ref> - 子孫は登美[[真人]]
:*子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など
== 関連項目 == *[[久米寺]]・・・来目王子が開基とされる。 *[[久留米市]]・・・地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 *[[新羅征討計画]]
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

== 系譜<small><ref>日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。</ref></small> == *父：[[用明天皇]] *母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 *同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]]
*妃：由波利王<ref>読みは「ゆばりのみこ」</ref>（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]]皇女 **男子：男王<ref>読みは「おとこのみこ」</ref> **女子：星河女王<ref>読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」</ref> **男子：佐富王<ref>読みは「さほのみこ」</ref> *妃：比里古郎女<ref>読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」</ref> - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 **男子：高椅王<ref>読みは「たかはしのみこ」</ref> *生母不明 **男子：日田王<ref>中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀寿男『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会,1986年 による）</ref> - 子孫は登美[[真人]]
:*子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など
== 関連項目 == *[[久米寺]]・・・来目王子が開基とされる。 *[[久留米市]]・・・地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 *[[新羅征討計画]]

[[日田王]] | 父親 = [[用明天皇]]（第31代[[天皇]]） | 母親 = [[穴穂部間人皇女]] | 役職 = [[征新羅大将軍]] | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。 __TOC__{{-}} == 略歴 == 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]の際、[[征新羅大将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 == 系譜日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。 == * 父：[[用明天皇]] * 母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 * 同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]] * 妃：[[由波利王]]読みは「ゆばりのみこ」（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]][[皇女]] ** 男子：[[男王]]読みは「おとこのみこ」 ** 女子：[[星河女王]]読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」 ** 男子：[[佐富王]]読みは「さほのみこ」 * 妃：[[比里古郎]]女読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」 - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 ** 男子：[[高椅王]]読みは「たかはしのみこ」 * 生母不明 ** 男子：[[日田王]]中田憲信『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会　1986年（昭和60年） による） - 子孫は[[登美氏|登美]][[真人]] :* 子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[久米寺]] - 来目王子が開基とされる。 * [[久留米市]] - 地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 * [[新羅征討計画]] {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

[[日田王]] | 父親 = [[用明天皇]]（第31代[[天皇]]） | 母親 = [[穴穂部間人皇女]] | 役職 = [[征新羅大将軍]] | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''来目皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。父は[[用明天皇]]。母は[[穴穂部間人皇女]]。[[聖徳太子]]は同母兄。 __TOC__{{-}} == 略歴 == 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]の際、[[征新羅大将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、同墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 == 系譜日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。 == * 父：[[用明天皇]] * 母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 * 同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]] * 妃：[[由波利王]]読みは「ゆばりのみこ」（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]][[皇女]] ** 男子：[[男王]]読みは「おとこのみこ」 ** 女子：[[星河女王]]読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」 ** 男子：[[佐富王]]読みは「さほのみこ」 * 妃：[[比里古郎]]女読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」 - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 ** 男子：[[高椅王]]読みは「たかはしのみこ」 * 生母不明 ** 男子：[[日田王]]中田憲信『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による） - 子孫は[[登美氏|登美]][[真人]] :* 子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[久米寺]] - 来目王子が開基とされる。 * [[久留米市]] - 地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 * [[新羅征討計画]] {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）}}]] 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月、[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]の際、[[征新羅大将軍]]として軍二万五千を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌年（[[603年]]）2月4日、筑紫にて[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山：[[桑山塔ノ尾古墳]]参照）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 == 系譜 == 日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。 * 父：[[用明天皇]] * 母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 * 同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]] * 妃：[[由波利王]]読みは「ゆばりのみこ」（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]][[皇女]] ** 男子：[[男王]]読みは「おとこのみこ」 ** 女子：[[星河女王]]読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」 ** 男子：[[佐富王]]読みは「さほのみこ」 * 妃：[[比里古郎]]女読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」 - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 ** 男子：[[高椅王]]読みは「たかはしのみこ」 * 生母不明 ** 男子：[[日田王]]中田憲信『皇胤志』（[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 による） - 子孫は[[登美氏|登美]][[真人]] :* 子孫：[[山村王]]・[[登美直名]]など == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[久米寺]] - 来目王子が開基とされる。 * [[久留米市]] - 地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 * [[新羅征討計画]] {{DEFAULTSORT:くめのみこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）}}]] 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月に[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]が立てられた際、[[征新羅大将軍]]として軍25,000を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌推古天皇11年（[[603年]]）2月4日に筑紫にて[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山：[[桑山塔ノ尾古墳]]参照）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 == 系譜 == 日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。 * 父：[[用明天皇]] * 母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 * 同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]] * 妃：[[由波利王]]読みは「ゆばりのみこ」（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]][[皇女]] ** 男子：[[男王]]読みは「おとこのみこ」 ** 女子：[[星河女王]]読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」 ** 男子：[[佐富王]]読みは「さほのみこ」 * 妃：[[比里古郎]]女読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」 - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 ** 男子：[[高椅王]]読みは「たかはしのみこ」 * 生母不詳の子女 ** 男子：[[日田王]]中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀[1986: 108]） - 子孫は[[登美氏|登美]][[真人]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 == 関連項目 == * [[久米寺]] - 来目王子が開基とされる。 * [[久留米市]] - 地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめ の みこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:用明天皇の子女]] [[Category:戦病死した人物]] [[Category:6世紀日本の人物]] [[Category:7世紀日本の人物]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}} [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}} In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. ({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Deaths from illness]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Prince Kume-qq]] [[Category:Prince Kume]][[Category:603 deaths]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:来目皇子]]

[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). ({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume.
