revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. [[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}}== See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. revision.text:
[[ko:구메노 황자]] {{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|12=draft}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|12=draft}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|12=draft}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=draft}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|12=draft}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|12=draft}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|12=draft}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|12=draft}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|12=draft}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|12=draft}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|12=draft}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|12=draft}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市|12=draft}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{draft categories| [[Category:Prince Kume]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Deaths from illness]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:603 deaths]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ko:구메노 황자]] [[sn:Prince Kume]] [[ja:来目皇子]] [[sn:rince Kume]] [[en:Prince Kume]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Prince Kume}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|12=draft}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|12=draft}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|12=draft}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|12=draft}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|12=draft}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|12=draft}} | royal_house = | father = {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} | mother = {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女|12=draft}} | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|12=draft}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}} }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|prince|ja|皇族|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}, also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}}. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}}
({{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}})}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|12=draft}}
({{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|12=draft}})}}]] In February 602, during the reign of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}}, Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|12=draft}} to lead a planned campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}} after its conquest of {{ill|Mimana|ja|任那|12=draft}}. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to {{ill|Chikuzen Province|ja|筑紫国|12=draft}} and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|12=draft}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in {{ill|Saba|ja|佐波郡 (山口県)|12=draft}}, present-day {{ill|Hōfu, Yamaguchi|ja|防府市|12=draft}}, and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|12=draft}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|12=draft}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=draft}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}. The site is currently managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=draft}}. * Father: {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|ja|用明天皇|12=draft}} * Mother: {{ill|Anahobe no Hashihito|ja|穴穂部間人皇女|12=draft}}, a daughter of {{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|12=draft}} * Siblings: {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|12=draft}}, {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|12=draft}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|12=draft}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|12=draft}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|12=draft}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|12=draft}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|12=draft}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|12=draft}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|12=draft}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|12=draft}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * {{ill|Kurume|ja|久留米市|12=draft}} – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{draft categories| [[Category:Prince Kume]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:Deaths from illness]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:603 deaths]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ko:구메노 황자]] [[sn:Prince Kume]] [[ja:来目皇子]] [[sn:rince Kume]] [[en:Prince Kume]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Prince Kume}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 皇族・貴族 | 人名 = 来目皇子 | 各国語表記 = | 家名・爵位 = | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 続柄 = 第31代[[用明天皇]]の第4[[皇子]] | 称号 = | 全名 = | 身位 = | 敬称 = | お印 = | 出生日 = 不詳 | 生地 = | 死亡日 = [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]） | 没地 = [[筑紫国]][[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]] | 埋葬日 = 不詳 | 埋葬地 = [[河内国]]埴生山岡上（現・[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） | 配偶者1 = [[由波利王]] | 配偶者2 = [[比里古郎]]女 | 配偶者3 = | 配偶者4 = | 配偶者5 = | 配偶者6 = | 配偶者7 = | 配偶者8 = | 配偶者9 = | 配偶者10 = | 子女 = [[男王]]
[[星河女王]]
[[佐富王]]
[[高椅王]]
[[日田王]] | 父親 = [[用明天皇]]（第31代[[天皇]]） | 母親 = [[穴穂部間人皇女]] | 役職 = [[征新羅大将軍]] | 宗教 = | サイン = }} '''来目 皇子'''（くめのみこ、生年不詳 - [[推古天皇]]11年[[2月4日 (旧暦)|2月4日]]（[[603年]][[3月25日]]））は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[皇族]]。'''久米王'''とも。[[用明天皇]]の子。 == 略歴 == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|来目皇子 [[塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|埴生崗上墓]]
（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]）}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[桑山塔ノ尾古墳|来目皇子殯斂地]]
（[[山口県]][[防府市]]）}}]] 推古天皇10年（[[602年]]）2月に[[任那]]を滅ぼした[[新羅]]に対する[[新羅征討計画]]が立てられた際、[[征新羅大将軍]]として軍25,000を授けられる。4月に軍を率いて[[筑紫国]]に至り、[[志摩郡 (福岡県)|島郡]]に屯営したが、6月に[[病気|病]]を得て新羅への進軍を延期とした。征討を果たせぬまま、翌推古天皇11年（[[603年]]）2月4日に筑紫にて[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]。[[周防国|周防]]の[[佐波郡 (山口県)|娑婆]]（遺称地は[[山口県]][[防府市]]桑山：[[桑山塔ノ尾古墳]]参照）に[[殯]]し、[[土師猪手]]がこれを管掌した。 [[河内国]]埴生山（はにゅうのやま）岡上に葬られた。現在、墓は[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]はびきの3丁目の[[塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|塚穴古墳]]（[[方墳]]・一辺約50m）に比定され、[[宮内庁]]の管理下にある。 == 系譜 == 日田王を除く4人の王子女は『聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文』所引『[[上宮記]]』による。 * 父：[[用明天皇]] * 母：[[穴穂部間人皇女]] - [[欽明天皇]]皇女・用明天皇異母妹、のち[[田目皇子]]（来目皇子の異母兄）妃・[[佐富女王]]母 * 同母兄弟：[[聖徳太子]]・[[殖栗皇子]]・[[茨田皇子]] * 妃：[[由波利王]]読みは「ゆばりのみこ」（[[桜井弓張皇女]]とも、婚姻前、もしくは来目皇子の死後[[押坂彦人大兄皇子]]妃か）- [[敏達天皇]]・[[推古天皇]][[皇女]] ** 男子：[[男王]]読みは「おとこのみこ」 ** 女子：[[星河女王]]読みは「ほしかわのひめみこ」 ** 男子：[[佐富王]]読みは「さほのみこ」 * 妃：[[比里古郎]]女読みは「ひろこのいらつめ」 - [[膳傾子]]女、[[膳部菩岐々美郎女|菩岐岐美郎女]]妹 ** 男子：[[高椅王]]読みは「たかはしのみこ」 * 生母不詳の子女 ** 男子：[[日田王]]中田憲信『皇胤志』（宝賀[1986: 108]） - 子孫は[[登美氏|登美]][[真人]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年 == 関連項目 == * [[久米寺]] - 来目王子が開基とされる。 * [[久留米市]] - 地名の由来を来目王子とする説がある。 {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くめ の みこ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の男性皇族]] [[Category:用明天皇の子女]] [[Category:戦病死した人物]] [[Category:6世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:7世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:6世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:7世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:6世紀生]] [[Category:603年没]]

{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}} | royal_house = | father = [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] | mother = [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|qq=}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prince|qq=}} of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}}
([[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]])}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]])}}]] In February 602, during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]], Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} to lead a planned campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]] after its conquest of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun|qq=}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in [[:en:Saba District, Yamaguchi|Saba]], present-day [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani no Inote|qq=}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. The site is currently managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}. * Father: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]], a daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * Siblings: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shigekuri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Matsuda|qq=}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Temple|qq=}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]] – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{translated page|ja|来目皇子|version=98815714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:603 deaths]] }} [[ko:구메노 황자]] [[sn:Prince Kume]] [[ja:来目皇子]] [[sn:rince Kume]] [[en:Prince Kume]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}} | royal_house = | father = [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] | mother = [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|qq=}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prince|qq=}} of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}}
([[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]])}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]])}}]] In February 602, during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]], Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} to lead a planned campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]] after its conquest of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun|qq=}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in [[:en:Saba District, Yamaguchi|Saba]], present-day [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani no Inote|qq=}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. The site is currently managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}. * Father: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]], a daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * Siblings: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shigekuri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Matsuda|qq=}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Temple|qq=}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]] – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{translated page|ja|来目皇子|version=98815714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:603 deaths]] }} [[ko:구메노 황자]] [[sn:Prince Kume]] [[ja:来目皇子]] [[sn:rince Kume]] [[en:Prince Kume]] [[Category:6世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:6世紀日本の皇族]] revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = Prince Kume (来目皇子) | native_name = | title = | image = | image_size = | caption = | succession = {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} | spouse = {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} | issue = {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}}
{{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}}
{{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}} | royal_house = | father = [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] | mother = [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|qq=}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prince|qq=}} of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}}
([[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]])}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]])}}]] In February 602, during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]], Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} to lead a planned campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]] after its conquest of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun|qq=}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in [[:en:Saba District, Yamaguchi|Saba]], present-day [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani no Inote|qq=}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. The site is currently managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}. * Father: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]], a daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * Siblings: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shigekuri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Matsuda|qq=}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Temple|qq=}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]] – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{translated page|ja|来目皇子|version=98815714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:6th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military His temporary burial was conducted in [[:en:Saba District, Yamaguchi|Saba]], present-day [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani no Inote|qq=}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. The site is currently managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}. * Father: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]], a daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * Siblings: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shigekuri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Matsuda|qq=}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Temple|qq=}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]] – the city's name is speculated to ([[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]])}}]] In February 602, during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]], Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} to lead a planned campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]] after its conquest of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}} | royal_house = | father = [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] | mother = [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]] | birth_date = Unknown | birth_place = | death_date = {{Death date|603|3|25}} | death_place = {{ill|Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun, Chikuzen Province|qq=}} | burial_date = Unknown | burial_place = {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山岡上|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]] }} '''Prince Kume''' ({{lang-ja|来目皇子}}, birthdate unknown – March 25, 603) was a Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|皇族|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prince|qq=}} of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]], also referred to as '''Kume no Ō''' (久米王). He was a son of [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]]. == Biography == [[File:Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino), haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's burial site: {{ill|Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|lt=Tsukaana Kofun|ja|塚穴古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukaana Kofun (Habikino)|qq=}}
([[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]])}}]] [[File:Temporary burial place of Prince Kume.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Kume's temporary burial site:
{{ill|Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|ja|桑山塔ノ尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwasan Tōno-o Kofun|qq=}}
([[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]])}}]] In February 602, during the reign of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]], Prince Kume was appointed {{ill|Seishin Dai Shogun|ja|征新羅大将軍|lt=Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishin Dai Shogun|qq=}} to lead a planned campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]] after its conquest of [[:en:Mimana|Mimana]]. Commanding an army of 25,000, he advanced to [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Chikuzen Province]] and encamped in {{ill|Shimogun|ja|志摩郡 (福岡県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimogun|qq=}}. However, he fell ill in June and postponed the expedition. He passed away on March 25, 603, without achieving his mission. His temporary burial was conducted in [[:en:Saba District, Yamaguchi|Saba]], present-day [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu, Yamaguchi]], and overseen by {{ill|Hani no Inote|ja|土師猪手|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani no Inote|qq=}}. Prince Kume was later interred at {{ill|Hanyū no Yama Kofun|ja|埴生山|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanyū no Yama Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|square tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} approximately 50 meters on each side, located in present-day [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]]. The site is currently managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]. == Genealogy == The genealogy of Prince Kume and his offspring is partially documented in the {{ill|Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|ja|聖徳太子平氏伝雑勘文|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōtoku Taishi Heishi-den Zakkambun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}. * Father: [[:en:Emperor Yōmei|Emperor Yōmei]] * Mother: [[:en:Princess Hashihito no Anahobe|Anahobe no Hashihito]], a daughter of [[:en:Emperor Kinmei|Emperor Kinmei]] * Siblings: [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]], {{ill|Prince Shigekuri|ja|殖栗皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shigekuri|qq=}}, and {{ill|Prince Matsuda|ja|茨田皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Matsuda|qq=}} * Wives and children: ''' Wife: {{ill|Yubari no Ō|ja|由波利王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yubari no Ō|qq=}} (also known as Sakuraibari Ōjo) '''* Son: {{ill|Otoko no Ō|ja|男王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoko no Ō|qq=}} '''* Daughter: {{ill|Hoshikawa no Hime|ja|星河女王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoshikawa no Hime|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Saho no Ō|ja|佐富王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ō|qq=}} ''' Wife: {{ill|Hiroko no Iratsume|ja|比里古郎女|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroko no Iratsume|qq=}} '''* Son: {{ill|Takahashi no Ō|ja|高椅王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi no Ō|qq=}} ''' Unknown consort: '''* Son: {{ill|Hita no Ō|ja|日田王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hita no Ō|qq=}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Tomi clan|ja|登美氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomi clan|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kume Temple|ja|久米寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kume Temple|qq=}} – believed to have been founded by Prince Kume. * [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]] – the city's name is speculated to derive from Prince Kume. {{translated page|ja|来目皇子|version=98815714|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Male royalty of the Asuka period]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Yōmei]] [[Category:7th-century Japanese royalty]] [[Category:6th-century military personnel]] [[Category:7th-century military personnel]] [[Category:6th-century births]] [[Category:603 deaths]] }} [[ko:구메노 황자]] [[sn:Prince Kume]] [[ja:来目皇子]] [[sn:rince Kume]] [[Category:6世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:6世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:7世紀アジアの軍人]] [[Category:7世紀日本の皇族]] [[Category:戦病死した人物]]
